target.title: Harden API rate limiting
target.body:
## Summary

This PR hardens ClawHub HTTP API rate limiting and makes HTTP route coverage systemic. In the installed Convex `httpRouter` surface, the route boundary is `http.route(...)`; there is no first-class middleware hook. The PR therefore installs one route-registration wrapper immediately after `httpRouter()` and before any app or auth routes are added.

The wrapper is the authoritative HTTP boundary. It applies a central policy instead of keeping a second inventory of handler-limited routes:

- `OPTIONS` preflights and auth metadata/JWKS discovery are intentionally unmetered.
- `GET` defaults to the `read` bucket.
- Non-`OPTIONS` mutations default to the `write` bucket.
- Downloads, exports, trusted publish token minting, package artifact reads, and npm tarballs are classified into their specialized buckets centrally.
- Existing handler-level `applyRateLimit(...)` calls become compatibility no-ops when the wrapper has already metered the same `Request`, so routes do not double-consume quota while the central policy remains authoritative.

The active limiter now uses Convex's `@convex-dev/rate-limiter` component. We define named fixed-window buckets for `read`, `write`, `trustedPublish`, `download`, and `export`, split by anonymous IP, authenticated API token user, and admin API token user. The component owns the live `rateLimits` table and sharding. The old in-app `rateLimitCounters` table is no longer used for active enforcement; its bounded prune job remains temporarily so rows from prior deployments can age out safely.

It also closes bypass classes where legacy API handlers and forwarded client IP headers could let traffic avoid quota enforcement. The intended contract is documented in `specs/rate-limiting.md`, `docs/http-api.md`, and the deploy notes for `TRUST_FORWARDED_IPS`.

## What Changed

- Router-level HTTP coverage: `convex/http.ts` uses `installRateLimitedRoutes(httpRouter())`; all later `http.route(...)` calls, including auth routes added by `auth.addHttpRoutes(http)`, are covered by default.
- Central route policy: `convex/lib/httpRouteRateLimit.ts` owns bucket selection by method and route family, with only intentional opt-outs for preflight and auth metadata.
- Component-backed quotas: `convex/lib/httpRateLimit.ts` uses `@convex-dev/rate-limiter` named buckets instead of ClawHub-owned status/consume mutations.
- Handler compatibility: `convex/lib/httpRateLimit.ts` tracks wrapper-metered `Request` objects so old in-handler limit calls return the same headers without consuming another counter.
- Header normalization: `convex/lib/httpHeaders.ts` merges headers case-insensitively so wrapper-applied rate headers and nested handler headers do not become duplicate comma-joined values.
- Legacy API routes: `/api/...` and `/api/cli/...` routes now rate-limit through the router wrapper before auth parsing, search work, upload ticket creation, publish/delete mutations, and telemetry writes.
- Package downloads: `/api/v1/packages/{name}/versions/{version}/download`, package artifacts, npm tarballs, and legacy download endpoints consume the `download` bucket.
- Client IP trust: anonymous traffic no longer trusts `cf-connecting-ip`, `x-forwarded-for`, `x-real-ip`, or `fly-client-ip` unless `TRUST_FORWARDED_IPS` is explicitly enabled.
- Missing-IP fallback buckets: anonymous requests without a trusted client IP use one fallback bucket per rate-limit kind, not caller-controlled paths, slugs, package names, versions, query strings, or artifact parameters.
- Legacy cleanup: `convex/rateLimits.ts` now only prunes old `rateLimitCounters` rows; active enforcement moved to the component.

## Route Scope

`vercel.json` rewrites `/api/:path*` to the Convex site deployment, so this PR affects Convex HTTP API traffic, not TanStack page routing directly. The local page smoke below verifies that SSR/browser pages still load against seeded Convex data.

Central policy classification:

- Unmetered: `OPTIONS /api/*`, OIDC metadata, JWKS.
- `read`: default `GET`, including v1 catalog/search/detail/list routes, legacy search/detail routes, whoami/auth-required read routes before they return `401`, users/transfers/content-rights reads, package/npm metadata reads.
- `write`: default non-`OPTIONS`, including publish, scan submit, package/skill mutations, stars, users, content-rights writes, CLI device/auth/publish/upload/delete/telemetry legacy writes.
- `download`: `/api/v1/download`, legacy download, package version downloads/artifacts, package-inspector artifact, npm tarballs.
- `export`: `/api/v1/skills/export`, `/api/v1/plugins/export`.
- `trustedPublish`: `/api/v1/publish/token/mint`.

## Local Safety Smoke

Ran a local fixture-seeded instance, not a production snapshot. The prod-snapshot helper path was blocked by local Convex deployment/env state, so I used an anonymous local Convex target seeded through `devSeed:seedLocalFixtures`.

Local URLs used:

- App: `http://127.0.0.1:3028/`
- Convex HTTP site/API: `http://127.0.0.1:3241`
- Convex cloud: `http://127.0.0.1:3240`

Page smoke on the app port:

- `GET /`, `/skills`, `/plugins`, `/publishers`, `/search`, `/stars`, `/management`, `/cli/auth`, `/cli/device`: `200`
- `/packages`: `307` to `/plugins`
- `/upload`: `307` to `/skills/publish`

API smoke on the Convex site port:

- `GET /api/v1/search?q=local`: `200`, `read` bucket (`3000`), no duplicate rate headers.
- `GET /api/v1/skills?limit=5`: `200`, `read` bucket (`3000`), no duplicate rate headers.
- `GET /api/v1/plugins?limit=5`: `200`, `read` bucket (`3000`), no duplicate rate headers.
- `GET /api/v1/packages?limit=5`: `200`, `read` bucket (`3000`), no duplicate rate headers.
- `GET /api/v1/whoami`: `401`, `read` bucket (`3000`), no duplicate rate headers.
- `GET /api/v1/download?slug=local-agentic-risk-demo`: `200`, `download` bucket (`1200`), no duplicate rate headers.
- `GET /api/v1/skills/export`: `401`, `export` bucket (`10`), no duplicate rate headers.
- `GET /api/v1/plugins/export`: `401`, `export` bucket (`10`), no duplicate rate headers.
- `POST /api/v1/publish/token/mint`: `400`, `trustedPublish` bucket (`3000`), no duplicate rate headers.
- `GET /api/search?q=local`: `200`, legacy route, `read` bucket (`3000`), no duplicate rate headers.
- `GET /api/skill?slug=local-agentic-risk-demo`: `200`, legacy route, `read` bucket (`3000`), no duplicate rate headers.
- `GET /api/cli/whoami`: `401`, legacy route, `read` bucket (`3000`), no duplicate rate headers.
- `POST /api/cli/upload-url`: `401`, legacy route, `write` bucket (`300`), no duplicate rate headers.

## Traffic Visibility

I checked available repo/CLI signals for current traffic impact. The repo has Vercel Analytics/Speed Insights installed in the frontend, but no checked-in CLI path that exposes per-route production request volume. A bounded `convex logs --deployment wry-manatee-359 --history 100 --jsonl` returned no events in the local CLI window. `convex insights --deployment wry-manatee-359` did not return output before I stopped the read-only command. So the safety assessment here is based on route inventory, local fixture smoke, tests, and existing CI, not production traffic histograms.

## Verification

Reviewer-checkable behavior:

- `convex/lib/httpRouteRateLimit.test.ts` verifies default method policy, central special buckets, prefix classification for package/npm downloads, auth metadata opt-out, central overrides, request marking, and no duplicate rate headers when nested handlers return preapplied limit headers.
- `convex/http.test.ts` verifies legacy routes are behind the router-level default limiter, auth sign-in routes are covered, package version downloads consume the download limit, and existing internally-limited v1 routes are not double-consumed.
- `convex/lib/httpRateLimit.test.ts` verifies trusted IP headers are ignored by default, missing-IP fallback keys are scoped only by rate-limit kind, anonymous/auth/admin bucket selection, component bucket config, and fail-closed behavior on limiter write conflicts.
- `convex/rateLimits.test.ts` verifies bounded cleanup for legacy `rateLimitCounters` rows.

Automated checks:

- `bunx vitest run convex/http.test.ts convex/lib/httpRouteRateLimit.test.ts convex/httpApi.handlers.test.ts convex/httpApiV1.handlers.test.ts convex/httpApiV1.ambiguousSkillSlug.test.ts convex/downloads.test.ts convex/lib/httpRateLimit.test.ts convex/rateLimits.test.ts` passed: 470 tests.
- `bun run ci:static` passed locally.
- `bun run ci:unit` passed locally: 3707 passed, 1 skipped.
- `bunx convex codegen --typecheck=disable` passed and generated the component API binding.
- `bunx tsc -p packages/schema/tsconfig.json --noEmit` passed.
- `bunx tsc -p packages/clawhub/tsconfig.json --noEmit` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.

Not passing locally:

- `bunx tsc --noEmit` still fails on pre-existing `e2e/prod-http-smoke.e2e.test.ts:94` because `lastError` is not defined. That file is not touched by this PR.
- `bunx convex dev --once --typecheck=disable` could not start because another local Convex backend from `clawhub-ghsa-csp-hardening-split` owns port `3240`; I left that process alone.

Not run:

- Local structured autoreview could not start earlier because the local reviewer engine rejected the auth token with `401 Unauthorized` before producing findings.
target.author: jesse-merhi
